message:
⚠️ fix: harden dynamic card timer sandbox (Copilot review) (#2954)

Addresses all 4 Copilot review comments on PR #2951:

1. Reject string callbacks — setTimeout/setInterval now throw TypeError
   for non-function callbacks, closing the eval-like code execution hole
2. NaN delay protection — sanitizeDelay() guards against NaN producing
   unintended tight loops (NaN delay → 0)
3. Fail-closed blocklist — timer names re-added to BLOCKED_GLOBALS so
   removing wrappers from scope falls back to blocking, not exposing
   raw browser timers
4. Timer leak protection — per-card timer registry with MAX_ACTIVE_TIMERS
   cap (20) and auto-cleanup on card unmount via cleanup callback

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
